title: file_picker
objectives: Pick single/multiple files with type filters
Handle iOS document picker + Android SAF
Deal with large files without OOM

sections.markdown: `file_picker` opens the platform document/file picker and returns file(s) — with optional MIME/type filtering, multi-select and byte streams for large content.
Set `withData: false` for large files — otherwise the whole content is loaded into memory (OOM risk). Use `f.path` + a `File(...)` stream, or upload via a resumable client.
Assuming a stable file path. Android SAF and iOS document picker often return content-URIs / temporary paths; copy to your app's docs directory if you need durability.
For image/video specifically, `image_picker` gives a nicer UX; use `file_picker` for arbitrary documents (PDFs, spreadsheets, archives).

flashcards.back: Loads the entire file into memory — OOM on large files.
No — copy to your app's docs directory if you need durability.

flashcards.front: Prefer image_picker vs file_picker for photos?
flashcards.back: image_picker for photos/videos; file_picker for arbitrary documents.

quiz.explain: Never load huge files into memory.
Copy to a durable path if needed.

challenges.text: Pick a single PDF and show its size.
Pick multiple docs with a type filter; render a list.
Stream a picked file to a server without loading into memory.
Copy the picked file to app docs; resume upload after app restart.

interviewQuestions: How do you avoid OOM on large picks?
